title: 3) Patient’s face and hands
text:
• The patient’s face should be without make-up and thoroughly cleaned.
• The patient’s hair should tidy so as to fully expose the face. Prepare a hair band or
towel to hold the patient's hair.
• The patient’s hands should be clean too, because they may touch their face often during
the treatment session.
• I recommend sterile alcohol pads instead of sterile alcohol cotton balls for cleaning the
patient’s face because the sterile alcohol pad is much thinner, meaning there is a smaller
chance of alcohol spilling over to near the eyes, nose, or mouth.
3C - Concentrate
